ID:       ddx-d3aca2b7
Title:    test: cover task execution cleanup and resource exhaustion end-to-end
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:2, area:test, area:agent, area:cli, kind:test, reliability, spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025, cleanup, resource-exhaustion, e2e
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-f3dfde0d
Deps:     ddx-5d154c5d, ddx-8fa7ecf6, ddx-2d49729e, ddx-7fe583dc, ddx-ef2a7638
Desc:     PROBLEM
The cleanup/resource contract needs an end-to-end regression harness, not only isolated unit tests. The May 6 failure crossed command, git worktree, tracker claim, resource classification, and operator-output boundaries: a unit-only suite could still miss the exact behavior where ddx work claims the next bead after ENOSPC.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:686-718 owns real git worktree setup/cleanup, while cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:437-530 owns queue drain loop state; existing tests cover many individual branches but not the full resource-exhausted incident path.
- cli/cmd/try.go:306-342 and cli/cmd/work.go:101 route command contexts into workers, but there is no command-level e2e test proving cleanup/resource failures produce correct operator output and tracker state.
- cli/internal/agent/run_state_test.go:95-145 tests orphan recovery shapes, but not full DDx temp root cleanup plus registered worktree pruning plus claim safety.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:449-498 requires cleanup to be inline, loop-level, background, observable, and loop-fatal on resource exhaustion.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add end-to-end tests that use a temp project, temp DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR, real git worktree commands where feasible, and injectable resource-check failures where filling a filesystem would be unsafe.
- Verify tracker state after failure: no extra bead claimed, failed bead open/re-claimable, no retry-after cooldown for host resource exhaustion.
- Verify cleanup state after failure: stale unregistered dirs and stale registered worktrees are removed; active/preserved evidence survives.
- Verify operator output and loop events contain cleanup summary and resource_exhausted stop reason.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not create a real ENOSPC by filling /tmp or exhausting inodes in CI.
- Do not require live harness/model/provider calls.
- Do not test unrelated production-reachability bead content.
Accept:   1. TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_StopsBeforeNextClaim runs ddx work against two ready beads with injected resource_exhausted setup failure and verifies only the first bead is touched.
2. TestWorkCleanupEndToEnd_PrunesStaleRegisteredAndUnregisteredWorktrees creates real stale DDx worktrees/dirs under a temp DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR and verifies ddx work or ddx cleanup prunes them safely.
3. TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_Reclaimable verifies ddx try on a target bead fails with resource_exhausted, exits non-zero, and leaves the bead open/unclaimed without execute-loop-retry-after.
4. TestCleanupEndToEnd_PreservesPublishedEvidence verifies complete .ddx/executions and .ddx/runs bundles plus refs/ddx/iterations/* are not removed.
5. TestBackgroundCleanupEndToEnd_UsesLockAndJitter verifies periodic cleanup can be driven deterministically with fake time and does not run concurrent destructive passes.
6. The e2e tests use temp directories and injectable fakes rather than consuming real disk/inodes.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd|TestWorkCleanupEndToEnd|TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd|TestCleanupEndToEnd|TestBackgroundCleanupEndToEnd" -count=1 passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

---SKILL---
---
name: ddx
description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.
---

# DDx

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
items with acceptance criteria), a task-execution boundary (DDx
orchestrates work while Fizeau routes providers/models, catalog lookups,
and transcript rendering), a persona system (bindable AI
personalities), a library registry (plugins with prompts, templates,
personas), and git-aware synchronization. This skill makes any
skills-compatible coding agent (Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini
CLI, etc.) understand and operate the DDx surface correctly.

## How this skill works

The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.

**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
guidance, not this overview alone.**

## Vocabulary

Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
exact definitions.

- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
- **Queue** — the set of open beads. The *ready queue* is the subset
  with all dependencies closed. `ddx bead ready`, `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
- **Blocked** — a bead with at least one unclosed dependency.
  `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
- **Run** — one task invocation atom. `ddx run` calls Fizeau `Execute`
  once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
  passthrough constraints.
- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
  finalization.
- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
  below).
- **Harness** — a Fizeau routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
  branch on harness names.
  Example: `ddx run --model qwen36 --prompt task.md` forwards `qwen36`
  unchanged; Fizeau owns the fuzzy match.
- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
  personas.
- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
- **Power bounds** — `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` integers passed to the
  upstream execution service. DDx may raise `MinPower` on eligible retries;
  Fizeau owns the concrete harness/provider/model routing.
  DDx passes raw `--model`, `--provider`, and `--harness` strings through
  unchanged when operators supply them.
- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
  `ddx install <name>`.
- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx does not specify subagent
  orchestration; that remains harness business.
- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
  `ddx upgrade`.
- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
  via `spec-id`.
- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
  at its governing artifact.

## Intent router

Before responding, read the matching file.

| User says / asks about | Read this file |
|---|---|
| write/plan work, "create a bead", "file this as work", bead metadata, acceptance criteria, dependencies | `reference/beads.md` |
| "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "run work", verify-and-close | `reference/work.md` |
| "review this", "check against spec", bead review, quorum review, code review, adversarial check | `reference/review.md` |
| "score a bead", "triage a failed attempt", "refine a bead", bead authoring lint | `bead-lifecycle/` |
| "run an agent", "dispatch", harness/provider/model passthrough, power, effort, "use a persona", role bindings | `reference/agents.md` |
| "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "how am I doing", health check, "ddx doctor", sync status | `reference/status.md` |

If the intent spans multiple files (e.g., "create a bead and then
run it"), read beads.md first, then work.md. If no match, ask the
user which concept they mean rather than guessing.

## Top-level policy reminders

These apply across all DDx operations. Do not restate them in every
reference file; do not violate them.

- **Never edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.** All tracker changes go
  through `ddx bead create/update/close/dep`. Direct edits corrupt
  bead history and cannot be audited.
- **Tracker changes are commit-worthy.** After `ddx bead create`,
  `update`, `dep add/remove`, or `close`, commit the resulting
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` change — either as a tracker-only commit or
  folded into the same commit as related implementation changes.
- **Preserve bead-attempt commit history.** Branches containing
  `ddx try` / `ddx work` execution commits carry an audit trail. **Never
  squash, rebase, filter, or amend** these commits. Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `git merge --no-ff` when merging.
  `gh pr merge --squash` and `--rebase` are forbidden on these
  branches.
- **Work in worktrees for parallel agents.** Use `wt switch -c
  <branch>` (worktrunk) or equivalent to give each concurrent agent
  its own isolated checkout. Execute-bead does this automatically;
  manual parallel work should too.
- **Power-first execution dispatch.** Default to `ddx run`/`ddx try`/`ddx work`
  with power bounds. `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough
  constraints only; DDx must not use them for routing policy.

## Links out

- Full CLI reference: `ddx --help`, `ddx <subcommand> --help`.
- Governing feature specs: see `FEAT-*` documents under your
  project's `docs/` tree — especially the CLI, beads, agent-service,
  executions, and skills features.
- Personas README: shipped by the default `ddx` plugin at
  `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md`.
- Open standard this skill conforms to:
  [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io).

data.text: The bead is already marked decomposed in its own record, so I’m checking the DDx work-flow guidance before I decide whether to implement or to leave an evidence-only rationale.

data.output:
# Work — Drain the Queue, Execute, Verify, Close

"Doing work" in DDx means draining the ready queue: pick the top ready bead,
run one or more `ddx try` attempts, verify the result, and close the bead on
success or leave it available for a future eligible retry.

## Default surface: `ddx work`

```bash
ddx work
```

`ddx work` drains the queue by picking ready beads and invoking `ddx try`.
`ddx try` wraps `ddx run`, which is the single task-invocation primitive. DDx
owns queue iteration, attempt evidence, and retry policy; Fizeau owns
provider/model routing.

Flags worth knowing:

- `--once` — pick one bead and stop (don't loop).
- `--poll-interval <dur>` — continuous worker mode; wait between
  iterations.
- `--min-power <n>` / `--max-power <n>` — requested agent power bounds.
- `--top-power` — choose a `MinPower` threshold from the agent catalog.
- `--harness <name>` / `--provider <name>` / `--model <ref>` — passthrough
  constraints only. DDx sends them unchanged and does not route on them. For
  example, `ddx run --model qwen36 --prompt task.md` forwards `qwen36`
  verbatim; Fizeau decides whether that raw string matches a catalog entry.

## Primitive: `ddx try`

For targeted re-runs, debugging, or running a specific bead:

```bash
ddx try <bead-id>
ddx try <bead-id> --from <rev>      # base commit override
ddx try <bead-id> --no-merge        # preserve result, don't land
```

`ddx try` runs one bead attempt in an isolated git worktree. It calls `ddx run`
for the actual agent invocation.

## Pick by default, not by ID

Under normal operation, **don't specify a bead ID**. `ddx work` picks
the top ready bead based on priority + dependency satisfaction. Only
pin a specific ID when debugging (`ddx try <id>`) or
when the queue ordering would pick the wrong bead and you need to
override.

## Verify independently before closing

**Agents hallucinate successful completions.** Do not trust the
agent's self-report. Before closing a bead:

1. Run the acceptance-criteria command yourself (from the bead's
   `accept:` field). If it's `go test ./foo/...`, run that.
2. Check the resulting commit against the in-scope file list from
   the bead description. Out-of-scope files touched? Reject the
   attempt.
3. Read the commit message — does it reference the bead ID?
   (`[ddx-<id>]` or similar in the commit subject is the convention.)

If all three pass: close the bead.

## Close on success, unclaim on failure

DDx try/work outcomes form a specific taxonomy. Each outcome
maps to a concrete follow-up action:

| Outcome | Meaning | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `success` | Tests pass, AC met, commit landed | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `already_satisfied` | No changes needed (AC was already green) | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `no_changes` | Agent returned without producing commits and wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `no_evidence_produced` | Agent exited without a commit or rationale | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate harness/commit failure |
| `land_conflict` | Merge conflict on landing the result | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `post_run_check_failed` | Tests or gate failed after landing | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |
| `execution_failed` | Agent subprocess errored (timeout, crash, provider error) | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `structural_validation_failed` | Result failed structural sanity check | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |

`ddx work` applies these actions automatically. If you're running
`ddx try` directly, apply them manually: `ddx bead update <id>
--unclaim` to release a bead after a non-closing outcome, so another
worker can pick it up.

**Never leave a bead half-owned** — every execution either closes or
unclaims.

## Testing expectations for bead implementations

When an agent implements a bead, its output should include tests
that exercise the new code:

- **Unit tests** for logic with in-memory stubs for first-party
  collaborators. Favor stubs over mocks; mocks that assert on call
  sequences test implementation, not behavior.
- **Integration tests** against real collaborators where the cost
  is small (real git in a temp dir, real DB in a temp file, real
  HTTP via a local test server).
- **Real e2e tests** at the outermost boundary. E2e tests that mock
  the database or the network are unit tests lying about their
  scope.
- **Coverage measurement only where the project tracks it.** Don't
  introduce coverage tooling just for one bead; use what the
  project already has. Coverage is a signal, not a target.
- **Performance claims require baselines.** If the bead's AC
  mentions "faster" or "scales", the acceptance test must include:
  (a) a numeric baseline, (b) an explicit boundary (what's
  measured, what's excluded), (c) a reproducible harness.

## Anti-patterns

- **Trusting the agent's "done"**: always re-run the AC command
  yourself before closing.
- **Closing on no_changes/no_evidence**: only `success` and `already_satisfied`
  close a bead. `no_changes` requires an explicit rationale. `no_evidence_produced`
  means the agent returned without a commit or rationale — unclaim and investigate.
- **Squashing bead-attempt commits**: the per-attempt history is
  an audit trail (evidence commits, heartbeats). Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `--no-ff`; never squash/rebase/filter.
- **Running passthrough pins without a reason**: power-bound dispatch lets the
  agent choose an appropriate route. Use `--harness`, `--provider`, or
  `--model` only for explicit operator constraints, bug repros, or controlled
  tests.
- **Parallel workers on the same claimed bead**: the tracker
  guards against this via claim semantics, but don't try to defeat
  it — each claim represents an in-flight attempt.

## CLI reference

```bash
ddx work                                    # default queue drain
ddx work --once                             # one bead, then stop
ddx work --poll-interval 30s                # continuous worker
ddx work --min-power 10                     # request stronger attempts
ddx work --harness claude                   # passthrough constraint

ddx try <id>                                # one bead attempt
ddx try <id> --from <rev>                   # override base commit
ddx try <id> --no-merge                     # preserve iteration
```

Full flag list: `ddx work --help`, `ddx try --help`, `ddx run --help`.

data.text: I’ve confirmed this is a parent bead that was already decomposed into five child beads. I’m checking whether a required evidence file already exists before I decide whether to add a minimal no-changes rationale or leave the execution trail untouched.

data.output:
ID:       ddx-07f2709c
Title:    test: stop ddx work before second claim on resource exhaustion
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:2, area:test, area:agent, area:cli, kind:test, reliability, spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025, cleanup, resource-exhaustion, e2e
Parent:   ddx-d3aca2b7
Deps:     ddx-d3aca2b7
Desc:     PROBLEM
The queue-drain loop still lacks a command-level regression that proves host resource exhaustion stops ddx work before it touches the next ready bead. Existing unit tests in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_resource_exhausted_test.go:52-100 verify the loop stops after one executor call, but cli/cmd/work.go:99-105 only routes into the worker and there is no end-to-end test that exercises the command boundary with two ready beads and an injected resource_exhausted setup failure. That leaves a gap where a future change could accidentally claim the second bead after ENOSPC-like failure.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:451-556 owns startup cleanup, pre-claim cleanup, and the main loop exit path. cli/cmd/work.go:99-105 hands ddx work straight into that loop, but no test currently proves the observable command behavior: exit after the first resource_exhausted attempt, no second claim, and the failed bead remains reclaimable.

PROPOSED FIX
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_e2e_test.go or a new sibling test file: add a command-level test that seeds two ready beads in a temp project and injects a resource checker / cleanup runner failure that returns resource_exhausted before the second claim.
- Assert the first bead is touched once, the second bead is never claimed, and the loop emits the resource_exhausted stop message.
- Verify the first bead stays open and unowned so it can be reclaimed later.
- Keep the harness in temp dirs and fakes; do not consume real disk or inodes.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new production behavior to the loop itself.
- Do not test unrelated provider/model routing, retry cooldowns, or no_changes paths.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_StopsBeforeNextClaim exists and runs ddx work against two ready beads with an injected resource_exhausted setup failure.
2. The test proves only the first bead is touched and the second bead is not claimed.
3. The test verifies the command emits the resource_exhausted stop reason and leaves the failed bead open and reclaimable.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_StopsBeforeNextClaim" -count=1 passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
Created:  2026-05-07 00:16:07
Updated:  2026-05-07 00:16:43

ID:       ddx-83f1f0af
Title:    test: keep ddx try resource exhaustion reclaimable
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:2, area:test, area:agent, area:cli, kind:test, reliability, spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025, cleanup, resource-exhaustion, e2e
Parent:   ddx-d3aca2b7
Deps:     ddx-d3aca2b7
Desc:     PROBLEM
The try command needs an end-to-end regression that proves a resource_exhausted failure leaves the target bead open and reclaimable instead of parking it behind execute-loop-retry-after or consuming the claim. Existing tests in cli/cmd/try_test.go exercise not-found, closed, unmet-deps, happy-path, and interruption, but they do not cover the command-level resource exhaustion path.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/try.go:324-333 checks the resource checker before the worker run and returns an ExitError on failure. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:695-715 turns a ResourceExhaustedError into a structured result, but there is no command-level test that verifies the failing bead remains open/unclaimed and does not receive a retry cooldown marker.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_Reclaimable in cli/cmd/try_test.go or a sibling e2e test file.
- Seed one ready bead in a temp project, inject a resource checker failure through the command factory, and run ddx try on that bead.
- Assert the command exits non-zero, prints the resource_exhausted stop message, leaves the bead open with no owner, and does not write execute-loop-retry-after.
- Use temp dirs and fakes only; do not fill real disk or inodes.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not test unrelated claim-approval or review flows.
- Do not add new retry-policy behavior.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_Reclaimable exists and invokes ddx try with an injected resource_exhausted failure.
2. The command exits non-zero, prints resource_exhausted, and leaves the bead open and unclaimed.
3. The test verifies no execute-loop-retry-after marker is written.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_Reclaimable" -count=1 passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

ID:       ddx-5ed78486
Title:    test: prune stale DDx worktrees during cleanup end-to-end
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:2, area:test, area:agent, area:cli, kind:test, reliability, spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025, cleanup, resource-exhaustion, e2e
Parent:   ddx-d3aca2b7
Deps:     ddx-d3aca2b7
Desc:     PROBLEM
Cleanup currently has command coverage for stale temp dirs and run-state files, but not an end-to-end regression that proves DDx prunes both stale registered worktrees and stale unregistered temp dirs safely under a temp DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR. The current cleanup tests in cli/cmd/cleanup_test.go:55-159 cover dry-run/apply and evidence preservation, and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_worktree_cleanup_test.go:48-65 only proves failed worktree setup removes a partial directory. That still leaves the real registered-worktree pruning path unexercised.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/cleanup.go:53-134 runs the production cleanup manager against the filesystem, but there is no command-level end-to-end test that seeds real git-tracked stale worktrees plus stale unregistered dirs and then proves the cleanup pass removes only the stale DDx-owned resources.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a cleanup end-to-end test in cli/cmd/cleanup_test.go or a sibling e2e test file that creates a temp project, a temp DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR, and real stale registered + unregistered DDx worktree directories.
- Use real git worktree registration where feasible; keep the rest of the environment in temp dirs and fakes.
- Run ddx cleanup --apply or the equivalent command path and verify stale DDx worktrees are removed, registered cleanup metadata is honored, and active/non-DDx paths remain untouched.
- Assert the cleanup summary reports the removed registered and unregistered counts that correspond to the seeded fixtures.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not validate evidence preservation here; that is covered by the sibling evidence-preservation bead.
- Do not introduce destructive cleanup of non-DDx directories.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestWorkCleanupEndToEnd_PrunesStaleRegisteredAndUnregisteredWorktrees exists and seeds real stale DDx worktree directories under a temp DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR.
2. The test verifies cleanup removes stale registered and unregistered DDx worktrees safely and leaves active/non-DDx paths alone.
3. The test checks the cleanup summary or command output reflects the pruned stale resources.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestWorkCleanupEndToEnd_PrunesStaleRegisteredAndUnregisteredWorktrees" -count=1 passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
Created:  2026-05-07 00:16:14
Updated:  2026-05-07 00:16:46

ID:       ddx-4fcd9427
Title:    test: preserve published execution evidence during cleanup end-to-end
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:2, area:test, area:agent, area:cli, kind:test, reliability, spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025, cleanup, resource-exhaustion, e2e
Parent:   ddx-d3aca2b7
Deps:     ddx-d3aca2b7
Desc:     PROBLEM
Cleanup must never remove published DDx evidence bundles, but the current command tests only prove a subset of that contract. cli/cmd/cleanup_test.go:129-159 proves stale temp dirs are removed while .ddx/executions and .ddx/runs examples survive, yet the acceptance requires explicit coverage for complete .ddx/executions bundles, complete .ddx/runs bundles, and refs/ddx/iterations/* remaining intact after cleanup.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/cleanup.go:64-134 delegates to the cleanup manager and prints the preservation summary, but there is no end-to-end test that seeds all three published-evidence forms and verifies cleanup leaves them untouched.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extend cli/cmd/cleanup_test.go or add a sibling end-to-end test that seeds complete .ddx/executions/<attempt-id> and .ddx/runs/<run-id> bundles plus refs/ddx/iterations/* under a temp git project.
- Run ddx cleanup --apply and assert the stale DDx temp dirs are removed while the published evidence remains on disk / in git refs.
- Verify the output or JSON report includes the preservation summary for the complete evidence bundles.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not require live provider/model execution.
- Do not remove or rewrite the published evidence artifacts.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestCleanupEndToEnd_PreservesPublishedEvidence exists and seeds complete .ddx/executions, .ddx/runs, and refs/ddx/iterations/* evidence.
2. Cleanup leaves every published evidence artifact intact while still removing stale DDx temp resources.
3. The cleanup output reports the preserved evidence bundles.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestCleanupEndToEnd_PreservesPublishedEvidence" -count=1 passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
Created:  2026-05-07 00:16:31
Updated:  2026-05-07 00:16:52

ID:       ddx-b2047bfb
Title:    test: drive background cleanup with fake time and lock serialization
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:2, area:test, area:agent, area:cli, kind:test, reliability, spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025, cleanup, resource-exhaustion, e2e
Parent:   ddx-d3aca2b7
Deps:     ddx-d3aca2b7
Desc:     PROBLEM
The background cleanup worker has separate unit coverage for periodic ticks and project-lock serialization, but no end-to-end regression that drives the worker deterministically with fake time and proves concurrent destructive passes do not happen. The current evidence lives in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go:203-351, while the production worker lives in cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_loop.go:25-180. That gap leaves room for a scheduling or locking regression that only appears when a long-lived process is polling cleanup in the background.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup_loop.go:25-180 owns runExecutionCleanupPass, jitteredCleanupDelay, and startExecutionCleanupWorker. Existing tests verify pieces of that behavior, but they do not drive the worker end-to-end with fake time/ticks while asserting the cleanup lock prevents overlapping destructive passes.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add TestBackgroundCleanupEndToEnd_UsesLockAndJitter in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go or a sibling agent e2e test file.
- Use deterministic tick/fake-time control so the worker can be advanced without waiting on real time.
- Run two cleanup workers or overlapping passes against the same temp project and assert at most one destructive pass runs at a time.
- Verify the worker still performs cleanup on the scheduled cadence and emits the expected cleanup observations without touching real disk.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add new scheduling policy beyond the existing jitter/lock contract.
- Do not consume real bytes or inodes.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestBackgroundCleanupEndToEnd_UsesLockAndJitter exists and drives the cleanup worker with fake time or deterministic ticks.
2. The test proves concurrent destructive cleanup passes do not overlap while the lock is held.
3. The test proves periodic cleanup still fires on the expected cadence under the deterministic clock.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestBackgroundCleanupEndToEnd_UsesLockAndJitter" -count=1 passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

Updated:  2026-05-07 00:16:54

data.output:
ID:       ddx-d3aca2b7
Title:    test: cover task execution cleanup and resource exhaustion end-to-end
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:2, area:test, area:agent, area:cli, kind:test, reliability, spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025, cleanup, resource-exhaustion, e2e
Parent:   ddx-f3dfde0d
Deps:     ddx-5d154c5d, ddx-8fa7ecf6, ddx-2d49729e, ddx-7fe583dc, ddx-ef2a7638
Desc:     PROBLEM
The cleanup/resource contract needs an end-to-end regression harness, not only isolated unit tests. The May 6 failure crossed command, git worktree, tracker claim, resource classification, and operator-output boundaries: a unit-only suite could still miss the exact behavior where ddx work claims the next bead after ENOSPC.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:686-718 owns real git worktree setup/cleanup, while cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:437-530 owns queue drain loop state; existing tests cover many individual branches but not the full resource-exhausted incident path.
- cli/cmd/try.go:306-342 and cli/cmd/work.go:101 route command contexts into workers, but there is no command-level e2e test proving cleanup/resource failures produce correct operator output and tracker state.
- cli/internal/agent/run_state_test.go:95-145 tests orphan recovery shapes, but not full DDx temp root cleanup plus registered worktree pruning plus claim safety.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:449-498 requires cleanup to be inline, loop-level, background, observable, and loop-fatal on resource exhaustion.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add end-to-end tests that use a temp project, temp DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR, real git worktree commands where feasible, and injectable resource-check failures where filling a filesystem would be unsafe.
- Verify tracker state after failure: no extra bead claimed, failed bead open/re-claimable, no retry-after cooldown for host resource exhaustion.
- Verify cleanup state after failure: stale unregistered dirs and stale registered worktrees are removed; active/preserved evidence survives.
- Verify operator output and loop events contain cleanup summary and resource_exhausted stop reason.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not create a real ENOSPC by filling /tmp or exhausting inodes in CI.
- Do not require live harness/model/provider calls.
- Do not test unrelated production-reachability bead content.
Accept:   1. TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_StopsBeforeNextClaim runs ddx work against two ready beads with injected resource_exhausted setup failure and verifies only the first bead is touched.
2. TestWorkCleanupEndToEnd_PrunesStaleRegisteredAndUnregisteredWorktrees creates real stale DDx worktrees/dirs under a temp DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR and verifies ddx work or ddx cleanup prunes them safely.
3. TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_Reclaimable verifies ddx try on a target bead fails with resource_exhausted, exits non-zero, and leaves the bead open/unclaimed without execute-loop-retry-after.
4. TestCleanupEndToEnd_PreservesPublishedEvidence verifies complete .ddx/executions and .ddx/runs bundles plus refs/ddx/iterations/* are not removed.
5. TestBackgroundCleanupEndToEnd_UsesLockAndJitter verifies periodic cleanup can be driven deterministically with fake time and does not run concurrent destructive passes.
6. The e2e tests use temp directories and injectable fakes rather than consuming real disk/inodes.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd|TestWorkCleanupEndToEnd|TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd|TestCleanupEndToEnd|TestBackgroundCleanupEndToEnd" -count=1 passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
